region_of_origin: Philippines (Potato Corner brand); served in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for curly or loop-shaped seasoned fries; closest parent 'french fries food product' (FOODON_00001709) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A039W (Potato products, fried) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for seasoned curly fries exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226803007 'French fries (food)' is the closest available term; no specific concept for curly or loop-shaped seasoned fries exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Mega Loopys is approximately 150 g, equivalent to one regular-sized box of curly fries as served at Potato Corner outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Potato chips, plain, salted (USDA SR Legacy #19411) adapted for seasoned deep-fried curly potato product; cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods entry for French fries, deep-fried (FDC ID 170720)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Mega Loopys is a proprietary Potato Corner product not listed in any approved primary nutritional database. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 170720 (Potatoes, french fried, all types, salt added in processing, frozen, as purchased) as the closest defensible analogue for a deep-fried seasoned potato product. Sodium is adjusted upward to reflect the additional seasoning powder typical of Potato Corner products. All values are per 100 g edible portion.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; French fries / fried potato analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal); University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists in approved sources specifically for Potato Corner Mega Loopys curly fries. GI of 75 is adopted from the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., AJCN 2021) for deep-fried potato / french fries (glucose reference = 100). This is the closest defensible analogous food. Glycemic load per 150 g serving = (75 × 41.0 × 1.50) / 100 = 46.3 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~720 mg per 150 g serving) from seasoning powder
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Mega Loopys due to the high sodium load from seasoning powder, which can contribute significantly to daily sodium intake and elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 75) and high carbohydrate content (~41 g per 100 g) leading to rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Mega Loopys because the high GI and substantial carbohydrate load cause rapid postprandial blood glucose spikes, complicating glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high total fat (15 g per 100 g), saturated fat (2.3 g per 100 g), trans fat (~0.1 g per 100 g), and high sodium from deep-frying and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of Mega Loopys as the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium from deep-frying and seasoning powder adversely affects lipid profiles and blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mega Loopys is a signature menu item from Potato Corner, a Filipino-origin fast-food brand with outlets across Southeast Asia including Singapore (Funan mall). The product consists of large, loop-shaped or curly-cut potato fries that are deep-fried in vegetable oil until crispy and then coated with one of several proprietary flavour powders — popular options include BBQ, cheese, sour cream, and chilli. The curly shape increases surface area, resulting in a crunchier texture compared to straight-cut fries, and allows the seasoning powder to adhere more evenly. Per 100 g, Mega Loopys provides approximately 312 kcal, 3.4 g protein, 15 g fat, and 41 g carbohydrate, making it a calorie-dense, high-carbohydrate snack. The sodium content is elevated at approximately 480 mg per 100 g due to the seasoning powder, rising to roughly 720 mg per 150 g serving. The glycemic index is high (GI ~75), meaning consumption leads to rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation. Clinically, this product is of concern for individuals managing hypertension (high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high GI and carbohydrate load), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium). It is best regarded as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary component. In Singapore, it is sold as a snack at SGD 6.50 per serving.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Mega Loopys?

confidence_reason: Mega Loopys is a proprietary Potato Corner product not listed in any approved primary nutritional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from the closest USDA FoodData Central analogue (FDC ID 170720, deep-fried frozen french fries) with sodium adjusted upward for seasoning. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 for deep-fried potato products. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 170720 (Potatoes, french fried, all types, salt added in processing, frozen, as purchased) as the closest defensible analogue. Sodium is adjusted upward to approximately 480 mg per 100 g to account for the additional seasoning powder applied at Potato Corner. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry used for this estimation. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry used for this estimation. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry used for this estimation. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry used for this estimation. 6. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 and confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for vegetable-oil-fried potato products with no animal-derived ingredients. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0; confirmed zero as this is a plant-based fried potato product with no animal-derived ingredients. 8. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (AJCN 2021) for deep-fried potato / french fries; no direct GI measurement exists for this proprietary product. 9. Allergen profile varies by seasoning flavour chosen; cheese and sour cream flavours contain dairy; some flavours may contain gluten or soy. Halal status is based on Potato Corner Singapore's general halal certification but may vary by outlet. 10. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this product is sold in Singapore. 11. is_vegan set to false as cheese and sour cream flavour options contain dairy; is_vegetarian set to true for non-dairy flavour options (conservative assignment for the base product).
